data.title: Pure Bioscience Shareholders Back Governance, Expand Stock Authorization
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Pure Bioscience ( (PURE) ) has provided an update.

Pure Bioscience, Inc. held its Annual Meeting of Stockholders on February 17, 2026, with approximately 66% of outstanding shares represented, and shareholders elected all seven director nominees with overwhelming majorities to serve until the next annual meeting. Investors also approved, on an advisory basis, the executive compensation program and ratified Weinberg & Company, P.A. as the independent auditor for the fiscal year ending July 31, 2026, signaling broad support for current governance and leadership.

In a key capital-structure move, shareholders voted to amend the company’s Certificate of Incorporation to increase authorized common stock from 200 million to 250 million shares, a step that provides added flexibility for future financing, equity compensation, or strategic transactions. No additional items were brought to a vote, underscoring that the meeting primarily reinforced existing governance arrangements while expanding the company’s capacity to issue new equity as needed.

The most recent analyst rating on (PURE) stock is a Hold with a $0.06 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Pure Bioscience stock, see the PURE Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PURE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PURE is a Neutral.

The score is weighed down primarily by weak financial fundamentals—ongoing losses, negative free cash flow, and a high-risk balance sheet with negative equity. Technicals also remain bearish with the price below key moving averages and negative MACD. Valuation offers limited support because the company is unprofitable (negative P/E) and no dividend yield is provided.

To see Spark’s full report on PURE stock, click here.

More about Pure Bioscience

Pure Bioscience, Inc. is a specialty chemicals company focused on antimicrobial solutions, developing and commercializing disinfectant and food safety products based on proprietary technologies. Its offerings target contamination control and hygiene in sectors such as food processing, healthcare, and institutional facilities, aiming to improve safety standards and regulatory compliance for customers.

Average Trading Volume: 41,072

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $6.27M

data.title: NaaS Technology Flags Nasdaq Market Value and Listing Standard Deficiencies
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

NaaS Technology ( (NAAS) ) has provided an announcement.

On February 20, 2026, NaaS Technology Inc. disclosed that it received a February 17 notice from Nasdaq indicating the company is not in compliance with the US$35 million minimum market value of listed securities requirement for continued listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market. The notice gives NaaS until August 17, 2026, to restore its market value to at least US$35 million for ten consecutive business days, during which time its Class A shares will continue trading under the ticker NAAS.

Nasdaq also noted that NaaS currently fails to meet additional listing standards tied to minimum stockholders’ equity of US$2.5 million and a net income threshold from continuing operations of US$500,000. While there is no immediate delisting impact, the deficiencies highlight mounting listing-compliance pressure on the EV charging service provider, and if NaaS cannot regain compliance by the deadline, its shares could face delisting, though the company may seek more time or appeal.

The most recent analyst rating on (NAAS) stock is a Hold with a $3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on NaaS Technology stock, see the NAAS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on NAAS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NAAS is a Neutral.

NaaS Technology’s overall stock score is primarily impacted by its poor financial performance, characterized by declining revenues, persistent losses, and high leverage. Technical analysis provides some stability with neutral indicators, but the negative MACD suggests bearish momentum. The valuation is unattractive due to a negative P/E ratio and lack of dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on NAAS stock, click here.

More about NaaS Technology

NaaS Technology Inc. is the first U.S.-listed electric vehicle charging service company in China and a subsidiary of Newlinks Technology Limited, an energy digitalization group. It provides new energy asset operation services by using advanced technology to match EV charging supply with demand, while helping charging stations and operators optimize efficiency and profitability.

Average Trading Volume: 21,870

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $2.15M

Find detailed analytics on NAAS stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

On February 20, 2026, Atossa Therapeutics entered into a new at-the-market offering agreement with Rodman & Renshaw LLC, allowing the company to issue and sell up to $50 million of its common stock through the agent on Nasdaq or other trading venues. Atossa plans to direct any proceeds toward advancing its clinical programs, funding working capital and general corporate activities, and potentially pursuing complementary strategic assets, while retaining full discretion to suspend or terminate the offering.

data.title: BriaCell Restructures Soluble CD80 Program via BriaPro Deal
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from BriaCell Therapeutics ( (TSE:BCT) ).

On February 18, 2026, BriaCell Therapeutics and its majority-owned subsidiary BriaPro Therapeutics announced an asset purchase agreement under which BriaPro will acquire BriaCell’s exclusive license to develop and commercialize Soluble CD80 for cancer treatment, along with related assets. As consideration, BriaPro will issue 23,972,589 common shares to BriaCell, raising BriaCell’s ownership in BriaPro to about 78%, with closing targeted for around March 12, 2026, pending disinterested shareholder approval and an independent valuation.

BriaCell will also provide BriaPro with a credit facility of up to $3 million to fund research and development of Soluble CD80, which has shown promising anti-tumor activity and immune-boosting effects in animal models. The transaction consolidates control of the Soluble CD80 program within BriaPro while deepening BriaCell’s economic stake, potentially sharpening strategic focus on this novel immunotherapy platform and influencing capital allocation and governance for shareholders of both entities.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:BCT) stock is a Hold with a C$5.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on BriaCell Therapeutics stock, see the TSE:BCT Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on TSE:BCT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TSE:BCT is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (no revenue, widening losses, and significant cash burn with likely ongoing financing needs). Technicals are mixed with some shorter-term strength but negative MACD and a price still far below the 200-day average. Valuation contributes limited support due to a negative P/E and no dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on TSE:BCT stock, click here.

More about BriaCell Therapeutics

BriaCell Therapeutics Corp. is a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing novel immunotherapies to transform cancer care, targeting patients who do not respond to existing treatments. Its majority-owned subsidiary, BriaPro Therapeutics Corp., is a pre-clinical immunotherapy company developing binding agents and proteins designed to enhance the body’s own cancer-fighting cells against tumors.

Both companies operate in the oncology-focused biotech sector, with BriaCell listed on Nasdaq and the TSX and BriaPro concentrating on early-stage research assets such as Soluble CD80. Their work positions them within the competitive immuno-oncology market, where innovation around immune system modulation is a key driver of long-term value and potential partnerships.

Average Trading Volume: 24,507

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: C$39.52M

On February 20, 2026, Entergy expanded its at-the-market equity distribution program by adding Barclays Capital Inc. as an agent and forward seller and Barclays Bank PLC as a forward purchaser under an amended Sales Agreement covering its common stock. data.title: Reliability Restructures Capital After Major Settlement Agreement
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

An announcement from Reliability ( (RLBY) ) is now available.

On February 16, 2026, Reliability Incorporated and its subsidiary Maslow Media Group entered into a settlement agreement with Vivos-related entities and individuals, resolving all remaining disputes stemming from prior arbitration and related litigation in Maryland. Under the deal, respondents agreed to transfer 253,292,210 shares of Reliability’s common stock back to the company, not challenge a key arbitral award, and grant mutual releases, with all money judgments to be marked satisfied and the arbitration and court case to be dismissed with prejudice once the transfer is confirmed.

The share transfer, characterized as a surrender in settlement of previously adjudicated claims, is expected to cut Reliability’s outstanding shares by about 84% to roughly 46,707,790, significantly reshaping its capital structure once completed. As of the latest report the company had not yet received transfer agent confirmation, but upon completion and filing of satisfactions and dismissals, management expects all pending disputes among the parties to be fully concluded, reducing litigation overhang for shareholders and potentially clarifying future corporate governance.

More about Reliability

Reliability Incorporated, a Texas-based corporation, is the parent of Maslow Media Group, Inc., a Virginia corporation. Through these entities, the company has been involved in staffing, media, and related business services, and has recently been engaged in extensive arbitration and litigation with various counterparties over ownership and control matters involving its common stock.

On March 31, 2025, VIP Play, Inc. amended and restated a discretionary, convertible revolving line of credit demand note with Excel Family Partners, controlled by its secretary and sole director Bruce Cassidy, increasing available borrowing to up to $14 million with a 12% annual interest rate and a conversion feature at a 20% discount to the lowest recent share price. The outstanding principal under this related-party note reached $23.29 million as of February 19, 2026, and on February 13, 2026 the board granted VP of Operations John Dermody options over 1.5 million shares vesting over four years, moves that deepen insider financing ties while further incentivizing senior management with substantial equity awards.

The financing amendment and rising borrowings may signal ongoing capital needs and increased balance-sheet leverage, while the discounted conversion right could dilute existing shareholders if exercised by Excel. The sizable option grant to Dermody aligns executive incentives with share performance but also adds to potential future equity overhang, underscoring VIP Play’s continued reliance on equity-linked instruments to support its strategy and operations.

data.title: SRx Health Solutions Receives NYSE Warning on Listing Compliance
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from SRx Health Solutions ( (SRXH) ).

On February 18, 2026, SRx Health Solutions received a public warning letter from NYSE Regulation stating it had violated Sections 301 and 713 of the NYSE American Company Guide. The breaches stemmed from issuing about 7.5 million common shares between December 31, 2025 and January 23, 2026 upon conversion of Series A preferred shares without proper listing approval or compliant shareholder authorization.

The exchange determined that SRx failed to obtain prior NYSE approval for the additional listed securities and that its written-consent shareholder approval was deficient under NYSE’s internal guidance on large share issuances. While all Series A preferred shares have since been converted or redeemed, the warning underscores the company’s noncompliance with continued listing requirements and signals that further violations could expose SRx to more severe regulatory actions, including potential delisting proceedings.

The most recent analyst rating on (SRXH) stock is a Hold with a $0.13 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on SRx Health Solutions stock, see the SRXH Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SRXH Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SRXH is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held down by weak financial performance (large losses, high leverage, and negative recent cash flow) and bearish technicals (below key moving averages with negative MACD). Valuation looks optically low but is less reliable given earnings quality, while recent corporate actions modestly offset risk via capital-structure simplification amid a higher-risk strategic pivot.

To see Spark’s full report on SRXH stock, click here.

More about SRx Health Solutions

SRx Health Solutions, Inc. operates in the healthcare sector and is listed on the NYSE American exchange. The company has recently engaged in equity financing involving Series A Convertible Preferred Stock, which can be converted into common shares, affecting its capital structure and share count on the public market.

SRx’s activities around preferred share issuance and conversion indicate an active use of capital markets instruments to raise funds and manage its balance sheet. These transactions have drawn scrutiny from the exchange, highlighting the compliance-sensitive nature of its financing strategy and its obligations under NYSE American listing rules.

Erie Indemnity Company ( (ERIE) ) has shared an update.

Erie Insurance announced on February 20, 2026, that president and CEO Tim NeCastro will retire on December 31, 2026, concluding a 30-year career with the company and a decade in the top role. The board will launch an immediate search for his successor, while NeCastro remains in place through year‑end to ensure continuity during the transition.

Under NeCastro’s leadership since 2016, Erie Insurance experienced significant growth to nearly $13 billion in premiums and more than 7 million policies in force, while maintaining strong financial results and its service-first culture. Following his retirement as CEO, NeCastro will continue to influence the organization and its hometown through his new role as president of the Erie Insurance Foundation, signaling a stable leadership handoff and sustained community engagement.

Coca-Cola ( (KO) ) has shared an announcement.

The Coca-Cola Company confirmed that Executive Vice President and Chief Operating Officer Henrique Braun will become chief executive officer on March 31, 2026, succeeding James Quincey, who will remain as executive chairman of the board. The leadership transition, initially announced on December 10, 2025, formalizes a separation of the CEO and chair roles while retaining Quincey’s strategic oversight.

On February 19, 2026, the company issued letters detailing compensation for both executives effective March 31, 2026, setting Braun’s base salary at $1.45 million with a target annual incentive of 200% of base pay, and Quincey’s base salary at $1.2 million with the same incentive target. Both leaders will continue to participate in Coca-Cola’s annual and long-term incentive plans and remain subject to share ownership guidelines, underscoring the company’s emphasis on performance alignment and continuity during the transition.
